reason: Provenance gap: the node certifies the Banach-algebra (completeness) claim solely by '(B(H)_sa is a complete order-unit space, GT-order-unit-norm)', but the cited locus GT-order-unit-norm = HOS joa-m.md:368-372 (section 1.2.1) does NOT support that B(H)_sa is complete. Lines 368-372 only DEFINE the term: 'If A is furthermore a Banach space with respect to the order norm then A is called a complete order unit space.' They make no assertion whatsoever about B(H)_sa, the operator norm, or completeness of any concrete space. Establishing that B(H)_sa actually IS a complete order-unit space requires facts located ELSEWHERE in HOS, none of which the node cites: (a) B(H)_sa equipped with the OPERATOR norm is a JC/JB algebra (3.1.2, joa-m.md:2300); (b) a unital JB algebra is a complete order-unit space whose order norm equals the given (operator) norm (3.3.10, joa-m.md:2552); and (c) B(H) is a C*-algebra in the operator norm hence complete (1.3.2, joa-m.md:460), so its closed self-adjoint part is complete. Citing only 368-372 for completeness is a byte-mismatch: the locus contains the DEFINITION, not the THEOREM that B(H)_sa meets it. Under the project rule 'no standard facts', completeness of B(H)_sa must be provenanced to 2300/2552/460, not 368-372.
